Reviewing the prototype?
Skip onboarding and load a fully populated demo athlete:
adapt

The plan that bends
around your life.

Every other endurance plan assumes you sleep 8 hours, never miss a workout, and have unlimited time. You don't. We get that.

Adapt is for working professionals and parents: people who want to finish endurance events without trying to live like full-time athletes.

Real-life examples
Bad night with the kids? Today's intervals ease.
Pool closed? Swim swaps to bike or run.
Travel week? Volume scales. No catch-up cramming.
Knee tight? Intensity adjusts automatically.

You'll still cross the finish line. Just on terms that actually work.

Already have an account? Same button. Log in on the next screen.

A bit about you.

So we can set safe intensity zones.

Please enter your first name to continue.

Your current fitness.

We'll go at a pace that matches where you are now. Be honest. This keeps you injury-free.

Rough estimate is fine. Helps us adjust intensity if your schedule doesn't leave room for full recovery.

Your goal.

Pick an event or just say "get fitter." We'll show you an honest timeline either way.

The plan adapts: hilly courses get hill work, hot races get heat acclimatization, open-water swims get sighting drills.
Targeting a time adds intensity work and sharper recovery rules. We'll check the goal is realistic for your hours. If not, we'll say so.

Your schedule.

Tell us the truth. We'll build around real life, not an imagined one.

Same training days most weeks, or does it change week to week?
No problem. We'll treat the days above as your typical week. When a week is different, tap any day on the calendar to set that day's exact time and which disciplines work.
Typical training disciplines for your event. Deselect any you don't have.
How much explanation do you want with your training advice? You can change this later.
Pick a vibe. You can change later.

How fit are you right now?

All optional. Pick anything you know, skip the rest. Concrete numbers let me prescribe real paces and zones instead of generic ones. Don't know any? That's fine. I'll start with conservative estimates.

A recent race time gives me your run-pace zones (Daniels VDOT). Skip if you haven't raced recently.
If you know either. Resting HR = morning bpm. Max HR = highest you've seen on a hard effort.
Honest answer beats best-case. The plan starts where you are.
FTP (bike) and CSS (swim): only if you've tested them. Skip if you haven't.
After you've trained for a week or two, I'll suggest a quick 5-min field test to refine these. For now, use what you know or skip.

Building your plan…

Applying Friel block periodization, Daniels VDOT pacing, and Seiler 80/20 to your inputs. This takes 15-30 seconds.

Your daily rituals
Prep
Morning check-in
15 seconds. Tells the engine how to shape today and adapt this week.
Weekly review
Sunday check-in
Tap below to see where you stand with your training plan. Updates live as you complete and adjust sessions throughout the week.
Race week prep
Activates in 40 days. Race week ritual: transition practice, course review, pacing strategy, and a pre-race mental prep checklist. We'll walk you through it as race day approaches.
Chat with your plan
Ask a question
Turn on AI Coach Chat

Coach Chat uses Mistral, an AI model, to give you open-ended coaching answers and adapt your plan in real time. Before you turn it on, here's what to know.

What gets shared: Your training plan, recent activities, and check-in data go to Mistral when you send a message. Your name doesn't.

Where it goes: Mistral is hosted in the EU. Adapt sends your data through its own server. No one else sees it.

Cost: Free during the prototype — 50 messages per day.

What it can do: Move sessions, shorten days, flag injuries, scale volume, and explain why. It cannot diagnose injuries or give medical/nutrition advice.

I'm your coach. Ask me anything about your plan, or tell me what to change. I'll explain the why and execute the change.
Plan didn't update after the coach said it would? Tap to force a rebuild.

Plan adjustments

Help & FAQ

How do I change my plan?
Tap any session on Today or Plan to view details. From there, tap Edit. Or talk to the chat: "move Saturday to Sunday."
What does my readiness score mean?
0-100 scale. 85+ is on track. 65-84 means small things to tighten up. Below 65 means real risks. Tap the pill anywhere to see the full breakdown.
What if I'm injured?
Say "my [area] hurts" in chat, or log it at morning check-in. Engine auto-adjusts. See a doctor or PT for actual medical advice. The app flags, it doesn't diagnose.
Can't run today? What do I do?
Tap "Adjust today" on the workout card. Deselect the disciplines you can't do. Workout adapts automatically.
How do I log a workout?
Workouts sync automatically from Apple Health on the native app. You can also tap any session on Today or Plan, then tap "Log this" to add manually (useful for sessions not recorded on a watch).
How do notifications work?
When your logged workout data suggests an adjustment, a notification appears on the bell. Tap Accept to apply, Reject to dismiss.
How does the chat work?
Coach Chat uses an AI model to answer your training questions and adjust your plan via tools (move sessions, shorten, flag injury, etc.). Structural changes show an Apply / Cancel card. You confirm before they take effect. Free during the prototype, capped at 50 messages/day.
I want to start over
Profile tab → "Start over with a new plan." Your current plan resets, you go through onboarding again.

Tell us when you can train and what each day looks like. Plan adapts around it.
💡 Toggle the day to set availability. Tap the discipline chips under each day to enable / disable. Example: keep Sunday for long runs only by leaving Run on and turning Swim/Bike off.

    About Coach Chat

    Open-ended AI coaching that can read and reshape your plan.

    What it does. Ask the coach anything about your training: why a session is structured the way it is, how to adapt when life interferes, what to expect on race day. It can also execute changes: move a session, shorten a day, flag an injury, scale a build week.

    What it sees. Each time you send a message, Adapt sends your current plan, recent logged workouts, and check-in data to Mistral (the AI). Your name and login don't go.

    What it doesn't do. No medical diagnosis, no nutrition prescriptions, no gear advice. It will redirect those.

    Daily cap. 50 messages per day per device, to keep API costs sane during the prototype.

    Reset. You can clear the conversation and turn off Coach Chat anytime in your Account screen.

    Privacy: Adapt forwards your messages and plan context to Mistral's API. Mistral states it doesn't train on API traffic. Conversation history is stored only in your browser.

    Adjust today's session

    Tell us what you have available (time and disciplines) and we'll rebuild a session that fits.
    Time available
    What can you do today?
    Adapted. 25 min
    Strength circuit
    3 rounds · Glutes, core, hip mobility
    25 min isn't enough cardio to meaningfully build aerobic fitness, but it's perfect for the posterior-chain work that most half IM athletes skip. This is the highest-value use of your time today.
